1.name: IN1
1.description: Input pin for LDO1. A 10 μF or greater input capacitor is required to assure robust operation.

2.name: IN1
2.description: Input pin for LDO1. A 10 μF or greater input capacitor is required to assure robust operation.

4.name: IN2
4.description: Input pin for LDO2. A 10 μF or greater input capacitor is required to assure robust operation.

5.name: IN2
5.description: Input pin for LDO2. A 10 μF or greater input capacitor is required to assure robust operation.

3.name: GND
3.description: Device GND. Connect both pins to the device thermal pad.

13.name: GND
13.description: Device GND. Connect both pins to the device thermal pad.

14.name: OUT1
14.description: Regulated output 1. A 10 μF or greater capacitor must be connected from this pin to GND to assure stability.

15.name: OUT1
15.description: Regulated output 1. A 10 μF or greater capacitor must be connected from this pin to GND to assure stability.

11.name: OUT2
11.description: Regulated output 2. A 10 μF or greater capacitor must be connected from this pin to GND to assure stability.

12.name: OUT2
12.description: Regulated output 2. A 10 μF or greater capacitor must be connected from this pin to GND to assure stability.

20.name: EN1
20.description: Enable pin for channel 1. These pins turn the regulator on and off. The ENx pin must be connected to INx if the enable function is not used.

6.name: EN2
6.description: Enable pin for channel 2. These pins turn the regulator on and off. The ENx pin must be connected to INx if the enable function is not used.

16.name: FB1
16.description: Feedback pin for channel 1. These pins are the inputs to the control loop error amplifier and are used to set the output voltage of the device.

10.name: FB2
10.description: Feedback pin for channel 2. These pins are the inputs to the control loop error amplifier and are used to set the output voltage of the device.

19.name: NR/SS1
19.description: Noise reduction pin for channel 1. Connect these pins to an external capacitor to bypass the noise generated by the internal band-gap reference.

7.name: NR/SS2
7.description: Noise reduction pin for channel 2. Connect these pins to an external capacitor to bypass the noise generated by the internal band-gap reference.

17.name: PG1
17.description: Open-drain power-good indicator pin for the LDO1 output voltage. These pins can be left floating or connected to GND if not used.

9.name: PG2
9.description: Open-drain power-good indicator pin for the LDO2 output voltage. These pins can be left floating or connected to GND if not used.

18.name: SS_CTRL1
18.description: Soft-start control pin for channel 1. Connect these pins either to GND or INx to allow normal or fast charging of the NR/SSx capacitor. If a CNR/SSx capacitor is not used, SS_CTRLx must be connected to GND.

8.name: SS_CTRL2
8.description: Soft-start control pin for channel 2. Connect these pins either to GND or INx to allow normal or fast charging of the NR/SSx capacitor. If a CNR/SSx capacitor is not used, SS_CTRLx must be connected to GND.

EP.name: Thermal pad
EP.description: Connect the thermal pad to the printed circuit board (PCB) ground plane.

VOUTx.conditions: max is VINx + 0.3 or 7.0, whichever is smaller

VSS_CTRLx.conditions: max is VINx + 0.3 or 7.0, whichever is smaller

VOUTx.conditions: 0.8 V ≤ VOUTx ≤ 5 V, 5 mA ≤ IOUTx ≤ 1 A

VDO.conditions: VINx ≥ 1.4 V, 0.8 V ≤ VOUTx ≤ 5.0 V, IOUTx = 1 A, VFBx = 0.8 V – 3%

ILIM.conditions: VOUTx forced at 0.9 × VOUTx(TARGET), VINx = VOUTx(TARGET) + 300 mV

IGND.conditions: Both channels enabled, per channel, VINx = 6.5 V, IOUTx = 5 mA

IGND_Both_channels_enabled__per_cha.conditions: Both channels enabled, per channel, VINx = 1.4 V, IOUTx = 1 A

ISDN.conditions: Both channels shutdown, per channel, PGx = (open), VINx = 6.5 V, VENx = 0.5 V

VIT(PGx).conditions: For PGx transitioning low with falling VOUTx, expressed as a percentage of VOUTx(TARGET)

Vhys(PGx).conditions: For PGx transitioning high with rising VOUTx, expressed as a percentage of VOUTx(TARGET)

VOL(PGx).conditions: VOUTx < VIT(PGx), IPGx = –1 mA (current into device)

INR/SSx.conditions: VNR/SSx = GND, 1.4 V ≤ VINx ≤ 6.5 V, VSS_CTRLx = GND

INR/SSx_VNR_SSx___GND__1_4_V___VINx.conditions: VNR/SSx = GND, 1.4 V ≤ VINx ≤ 6.5 V, VSS_CTRLx = VINx

PSRR.conditions: f = 500 kHz, VINx = 3.8 V, VOUTx = 3.3 V, IOUTx = 750 mA, CNR/SSx = 10 nF, CFFx = 10 nF

Vn.conditions: BW = 10 Hz to 100 kHz, VINx = 1.8 V, VOUTx = 0.8 V, IOUTx = 1.0 A, CNR/SSx = 1 µF, CFFx = 100 nF
